# CSBP | Codex Shared Best Practice

Name: CSBP
Subtitle: Codex Shared Best Practice
Type: Three-file Codex practice protocol kit
URL: https://xxyoudeadpunkxx.github.io/CSBP-Codex-Shared-Best-Practice/
Repository: https://github.com/XxYouDeaDPunKxX/CSBP-Codex-Shared-Best-Practice
Runtime: Codex operational practice layer

## Purpose
CSBP keeps recurring Codex best practices explicit, reviewed, and separate from automatic memory or AGENTS.md.
It is a review gate for practices that may deserve to persist.
It is not a memory that writes itself.

## Position
CSBP is a controlled middle layer:
- below operator instruction
- below AGENTS.md
- below local project instructions or project-local guidance
- separate from chat history
- controlled by the operator

## Authority order
operator instruction > AGENTS.md > local project instructions or project-local guidance > CSBP

CSBP never overrides higher-authority instructions.
If a CSBP practice conflicts with stronger instructions, the CSBP item is not applied.

## Three files
- CSBP-entry-point.txt: bootstrap and routing layer; defines system identity, authority order, usage modes, read order, and write restrictions.
- shared-best-practice.txt: runtime layer; stores promoted practices only. Active practices may guide work when relevant.
- shared-best-practice-compiler.txt: formation layer; evaluates, normalizes, promotes, revises, deprecates, removes, or rejects candidate practices.

## Install layout
.codex/memories/CSBP-entry-point.txt
.codex/memories/shared-best-practice.txt
.codex/memories/shared-best-practice-compiler.txt

Wire CSBP-entry-point.txt into .codex/AGENTS.md so a fresh Codex session reads it before using CSBP.

## Runtime behavior
During normal work, CSBP does not create new practices.
It applies already-promoted active practices only when their applies_when condition matches the current work.
If there are no active relevant practices, Codex continues without CSBP runtime guidance.
No active match means no forced behavior.

## Formation path
1. Operator raises or asks for a candidate.
2. Codex evaluates recurrence, scope, actionability, and authority.
3. The candidate is normalized into runtime block shape.
4. Weak or local material is rejected.
5. Valid material is proposed.
6. The operator approves or rejects.
7. Only approved practice enters shared-best-practice.txt.

## Admission criteria
A valid CSBP practice should be recurring, actionable, preventive, broader than one project or one session, compatible with higher-authority instructions, short enough to apply during real work, and likely to reduce recurring waste or error.

## Rejection criteria
Reject material that is one-off, project-specific, session-specific, vague, diagnostic-only, recovery-only, policy-like, explanation-like, already covered by AGENTS.md, already covered by local instructions, dependent on hidden or unstable context, or only an operator preference without stable operational need.

## Compatibility
If CSBP is the persistent best-practice layer, avoid running it alongside automatic memory systems that can also turn session carryover into future behavior. Two persistence layers create unclear authority.

## Keywords
Codex, Codex best practices, operational practice, review gate, AGENTS.md, shared best practice, explicit persistence, operator approval, CSBP
